articles.title: Florida Settles Lawsuit After 6-Year-Old Killed in Go-Kart Crash
articles.summary: Florida has reached a settlement with the family of a 6-year-old child who was killed in a go-kart accident at an entertainment center. The case is a stark reminder for FEC operators of the critical importance of age and height requirements, safety barriers, and ride supervision protocols. Operators should review their liability coverage and safety procedures in light of this high-profile settlement.

articles.title: Disney Reveals AI Plans for Theme Parks: Labor Forecasting and Personalized Guest Experiences
articles.summary: Disney's new CEO Josh D'Amaro and CFO outlined plans to use AI for trip planning personalization and precision labor demand forecasting across theme parks. The labor forecasting initiative aims to improve guest experience, employee satisfaction, and cost management simultaneously. FEC operators should take note as these AI-driven workforce and personalization strategies will increasingly trickle down to smaller venue operators via third-party platforms.

articles.title: Six Flags Posts 12% Revenue Increase in Q1 2026 on Higher Attendance and Per Capita Spending
articles.summary: Six Flags reported Q1 2026 revenue of $225.6 million, up 12% year-over-year, driven by a 4% attendance increase and 6% growth in per capita spending. While the company still posted a seasonal net loss of $269 million, adjusted EBITDA loss improved by $48 million. The strong per capita spending growth is an encouraging signal for the broader entertainment industry heading into the summer season.

articles.title: WhiteWater Reports 60+ Water Attraction Project Openings Worldwide in Record Year
articles.summary: WhiteWater, the world's largest water attraction designer and manufacturer, is highlighting more than 60 project openings globally across its portfolio of brands including FlowRider and Endless Surf. The milestone underscores strong capital investment in aquatic entertainment despite broader economic uncertainty. FEC operators with waterpark components or considering aquatic additions should watch WhiteWater's product pipeline closely.

articles.title: Foundations Entertainment University Schedules Tennessee Industry Training Event
articles.summary: Foundations Entertainment University, a leading educational resource for FEC operators, has announced an upcoming training event in Tennessee. These workshops are designed specifically for family entertainment center owners and managers covering business planning, revenue optimization, and operational best practices. FEC professionals in the region should consider attending to sharpen their skills and network with peers.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Announced for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, signaling continued growth in the FEC sector in secondary markets across the Southeast. The announcement reflects ongoing consumer demand for local indoor entertainment options in suburban communities. Existing FEC operators in the region should monitor the development as competitive landscape shifts.

articles.title: New FEC 'Gameland' Opens in Cookstown with VR, Sim Racing, and Retro Gaming
articles.summary: A new family entertainment venue called Gameland has opened in Cookstown, offering a mix of virtual reality, sim racing, and retro gaming attractions. The concept reflects a growing trend of blending classic arcade nostalgia with cutting-edge immersive technology to attract a broad demographic. This multi-format approach is increasingly popular with FEC developers seeking to differentiate in competitive markets.

articles.title: Frontgrid Supplies ParadropVR Motion Pods to National Naval Aviation Museum
articles.summary: Frontgrid's ParadropVR Pods — motion-based platforms that combine physical movement with VR — will be installed at the National Naval Aviation Museum in Florida, with an expected summer 2026 opening. The family-accessible VR experience is designed to complement existing exhibits by delivering a high-fidelity flight sensation. This deployment demonstrates how VR motion attractions can enhance institutional venues and offers a compelling case study for FEC operators considering VR investments.

articles.title: Ellis & Associates Launches AI-Supported eLearning Course on Drowning Detection for Aquatic Facilities
articles.summary: Ellis & Associates has released a new AI-assisted online training course called 'Scanning and Drowning Detection: Improving Recognition,' available at jellis.com and using real-time video footage to train lifeguards. The course represents a significant advancement in aquatic safety training, making high-quality instruction more accessible to waterpark and FEC operators. Venues with pools, splash pads, or aquatic attractions should evaluate this tool as part of their mandatory safety training protocols.

articles.title: P2:3 Consulting Partners with Aqua Tile to Improve Safety and Maintenance at Aquatic FECs
articles.summary: P2:3 Consulting has announced a partnership with Aqua Tile to provide safer, more comfortable, and lower-maintenance wet-area surfacing solutions for waterpark operators. The partnership targets common pain points including aging infrastructure, slip hazards, and ongoing maintenance costs in aquatic environments. FEC operators running splash zones, waterparks, or pool areas should consider this surfacing solution as part of routine facility upgrades.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named One of USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has been recognized by USA Today as one of the most trusted brands by parents, a significant reputational milestone for the FEC chain following its bankruptcy and rebranding efforts in recent years. Brand trust is a critical driver of repeat visitation and party booking for family entertainment concepts. The recognition highlights the power of targeted marketing and experience improvements in rebuilding consumer confidence.

articles.title: Semantic's LOOP Platform Offers Multi-Brand CMS Solution for Attraction Operators
articles.summary: Semantic has developed the LOOP platform, a single CMS that allows attraction operators to manage multiple brands, locations, and websites from one scalable system. The platform handles core functions like booking and security centrally while keeping each brand's visual identity flexible. Multi-location FEC operators looking to reduce digital fragmentation and IT overhead should evaluate this type of centralized web management solution.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Location in Coral Springs Shuts Down
articles.summary: A Monster Mini Golf franchise location in Coral Springs, Florida has permanently closed this week, adding to concerns about the viability of standalone mini golf concepts in competitive indoor entertainment markets. The closure may reflect broader challenges facing franchise FEC models that rely heavily on a single attraction category. Operators should review their attraction mix and revenue diversification strategies in response to evolving consumer expectations.

articles.title: 'Skate Central' Family Entertainment Center Coming to Beckley, West Virginia
articles.summary: A new FEC called Skate Central is planned for Beckley, West Virginia, bringing a skating-focused entertainment concept to a smaller market. The development is part of a broader trend of FEC investment in underserved secondary and tertiary markets where competition is limited. For suppliers and franchisors, smaller-market openings represent an expanding opportunity beyond major metro areas.

articles.title: Kidtopia Indoor Playground with Obstacle Courses and Climbing Coming to Dublin, California
articles.summary: Kidtopia, a new indoor playground featuring obstacle courses and climbing structures, is set to open in Dublin, California. The concept targets younger children and families seeking structured active play in a safe indoor environment. This type of facility represents a growing niche within the FEC sector, appealing to parents seeking alternatives to screen-based entertainment.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline and Family Fun Venue Opens at Rivergate in Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street, a high-energy family entertainment venue, has opened at the Rivergate retail and lifestyle center in Cape Town, South Africa. The venue brings trampoline parks and active entertainment to a growing African market, reflecting the global expansion of the FEC format. International expansion into emerging markets is an increasingly attractive avenue for established FEC brands and concepts.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A proposed claw arcade facility is going before a city planning panel, highlighting the regulatory steps that specialty arcade concepts must navigate before opening. Claw machine arcades have become a standalone business category, riding the wave of viral social media content around prize-redemption gaming. FEC operators and investors tracking emerging arcade formats should monitor the approval process as a bellwether for this growing concept.

articles.title: Six Flags Announces Leadership Transitions
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation has announced changes to its leadership team, signaling potential strategic shifts for the large-format theme park operator. Leadership transitions at major park companies often precede changes in expansion strategy, technology investments, or operational priorities that can ripple across the supplier and partner ecosystem. FEC industry stakeholders should track how new leadership shapes the company's direction post-merger integration.

articles.title: Katapult Delivers Design for New Gladiators Experience at UK's NEC
articles.summary: Guest experience design agency Katapult has helped launch the Gladiators Experience at Birmingham's NEC, an attraction based on the popular TV show that lets visitors take on iconic physical challenges. The experience blends active entertainment, museum exhibits, and photo opportunities into a branded IP attraction format. FEC operators interested in IP-based entertainment concepts can draw lessons from this collaboration between a design agency and major entertainment brands.

articles.title: Bókun Launches Launchpad Competition for Experience Operators in Europe
articles.summary: Bókun, a Tripadvisor-owned booking and channel management platform, has opened a competition for European experience operators to win a $10,000 prize, 12 months of free subscription, and commission-free distribution on Viator and GetYourGuide. The competition is open to tours, activities, and attractions and offers significant distribution and marketing value to the winner. FEC operators in Europe seeking to grow their online booking and OTA presence should consider applying.

articles.title: India's Indoor Entertainment Market Shows Strong Growth Trends
articles.summary: A new report highlights expanding trends in India's indoor entertainment market, reflecting rising middle-class consumer spending on leisure and recreation. The Indian market is emerging as a significant growth opportunity for FEC concepts, with demand for bowling, arcades, trampoline parks, and immersive attractions rising in urban centers. Suppliers and international FEC operators looking to expand into new markets should closely follow India's developing entertainment sector.

articles.title: Samsung Spatial Signage Transforms Queue Experience at Everland Theme Park
articles.summary: Samsung's Spatial Signage digital display technology has been deployed at Everland, South Korea's largest theme park, to enhance the guest queue experience with immersive content. The application demonstrates how large-format digital signage can transform traditionally passive wait times into engaging brand experiences. FEC operators investing in queue management and in-venue entertainment should consider how spatial display technology could reduce perceived wait times and boost guest satisfaction.

articles.title: World's First Underwater Theme Park Ride Opens at Aquarabia in Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: Sub Sea Systems has opened Aquaticar: Legend of the Glowing Guardian at Aquarabia, Qiddiya City — billed as the world's first underwater adventure ride where guests pilot bubble-powered submersible vehicles through an immersive story environment. The attraction sets a new benchmark for experiential water park design and storytelling in the attractions industry. FEC operators and park developers should note the growing consumer appetite for unique, narrative-driven experiences that go beyond traditional ride formats.

articles.title: $400 Million Oasis Resort at Lake of the Ozarks Installs First Ride as Development Progresses
articles.summary: Oasis at Lake of the Ozarks, a massive $400 million family resort and entertainment district in Missouri, has installed its first amusement ride — a 100-foot tower drop called The Plunge — with 11 more rides planned throughout the summer. The large-scale development represents significant capital investment in the Midwest entertainment market. Suppliers, ride manufacturers, and regional FEC operators should monitor this project as it will reshape leisure competition in the area.

articles.title: Paultons Park's £12 Million Viking-Themed Land 'Valgard' to Open May 16 with New Inverting Coaster
articles.summary: Paultons Park in Hampshire, England is opening Valgard — Realm of the Vikings on May 16, its largest themed land to date at a cost of £12 million, featuring the park's first inverting coaster and a new themed dining area. The project is a strong case study in themed land development that integrates new ride hardware, rebranded existing attractions, and immersive food and beverage offerings. FEC designers and park operators can draw valuable lessons from Paultons' phased approach to experience expansion.

articles.title: Amoeba Bengaluru Relaunches as Next-Gen Sports Bar with Indoor Entertainment Focus
articles.summary: Amoeba, a well-known entertainment venue in Bengaluru, India, has relaunched as a next-generation sports bar concept blending food, beverages, and active entertainment. The rebrand reflects a broader trend of adult-focused FEC concepts merging bar and dining experiences with interactive entertainment. Operators considering hybrid bar-entertainment formats should note this shift as consumer appetite for eatertainment continues to grow globally.

articles.title: LEGO Festival Returns to LEGOLAND California for Second Year Running July 20–August 30
articles.summary: LEGOLAND California Resort is hosting its second annual LEGO Festival from July 20 to August 30, 2026, offering families a creativity-focused event designed to drive summer visitation. The returning event format demonstrates the value of annual themed programming in sustaining repeat attendance. FEC operators should consider how seasonal event calendars can be used to drive visitation during peak summer periods.

articles.title: Queensbury Amusement Center Conversion to Distillery Signals Shifting Retail-Entertainment Dynamics
articles.summary: A former amusement center in Queensbury is being proposed for conversion into a distillery, illustrating how underperforming entertainment real estate is being repurposed for other uses. The story raises questions about the long-term viability of certain entertainment formats in specific markets and the importance of site selection and concept differentiation. FEC investors and operators should consider how evolving market conditions can affect the value and adaptability of their venues.
